data.title: Crypto market recovers with RIVER token surging 393% in the week of Jan 19-25. DeFi sector sees momentum rise.
data.description: In Panama City, by January 30, 2026, the cryptocurrency market experienced a slight rebound in the week of January 19-25, despite ongoing fluctuations. Investor confidence grew, prompting a shift of funds towards specific sectors with more solid narratives and practical applications. This movement indicates a change in sentiment as the market adapts to the uncertain conditions.

data.title: Aquaporin withdraws offering, plans insolvency proceedings. Jan. 30, 2026. Board of Directors decision.
data.description: Aquaporin A/S, based in Kongens Lyngby, Denmark, under company registration no. DK28315694, released announcement No. 04/2026 on Jan. 30, 2026. The Board of Directors disclosed inside information regarding the Company. Aquaporin A/S is located at Nymøllevej 78 and operates the website aquaporin.com.

data.title: Lexicon sets public offering at $1.30/share for 32M shares, with gross proceeds of $41.6M, option for 4.8M additional shares.
data.description: Lexicon Pharmaceuticals, Inc. (Nasdaq: LXRX) based in The Woodlands, Texas, disclosed the pricing details of their recent underwritten public offering. They announced the offering of 32,000,000 shares of common stock at a public offering price of $1.30 per share, with an estimated gross proceeds of $41.6 million, excluding underwriting discounts, commissions, and other expenses. The offering, which is entirely from Lexicon, is set to conclude around February 2, 2026, pending standard closing conditions. Additionally, Lexicon has provided the underwriters a 30-day option to purchase up to 4,800,000 additional shares of common stock at the same public offering price, after deductions for underwriting discounts and commissions.

data.title: Cathie Wood warns gold is in a speculative bubble with market cap at Great Depression levels, signaling impending collapse.
data.description: ARK Invest's Cathie Wood and former IIF Chief Economist Robin Brooks are cautioning about a possible speculative bubble in gold, citing the historic rally as a concerning signal. Wood warns that gold's recent surge is not reflective of stability but rather a sign of a cycle's conclusion with the market cap of gold as a percentage of the US money supply (M2) hitting levels unseen since the Great Depression. Wood highlights similarities to the crisis in 1934, where the dollar devalued against gold by nearly 70%, private ownership of gold was banned, and M2 crumbled. Wood emphasizes that the current bubble isn't in technology but in precious metals, expressing concerns about a potential significant fall in gold prices if the dollar strengthens, comparing it to the period from 1980 to 2000 when gold prices plummeted over 60%. This analysis hints at a possible downturn for gold's price in the near future.
